articles.summary: Home Minister Amit Shah accused the previous Congress government in Assam of allowing infiltrators to encroach on Kaziranga National Park and failing to protect its wildlife. He claimed that the current NDA government has successfully cleared the park of encroachments and improved security for the state's iconic one-horned rhinoceros.

articles.headline: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy invites Kerala CM to study state progress
articles.summary: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy has invited Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan to visit the state and see the progress made in development and welfare schemes over the past year. Reddy highlighted the state's achievements in sectors like agriculture and job creation, inviting a comparison with the performance of the Kerala government.

articles.headline: Kerala protests led to FCRA Bill deferment, says Prakash Karat
articles.summary: CPI(M) leader Prakash Karat stated that the Centre's decision to defer amendments to the FCRA Bill was due to widespread protests, particularly in Kerala. He alleged that the proposed changes were aimed at targeting NGOs that do not align with the government's political stance. The protests have highlighted concerns over the impact on social and institutional work.

articles.headline: Political content creators concerned over proposed new IT rules
articles.summary: Content creators and satirists in India have raised concerns over proposed amendments to the IT rules that could bring their work under stricter government oversight. They fear that the new rules could lead to increased censorship and limit free expression on social media platforms. The government has stated that the changes are needed to address misinformation and fake news.

articles.headline: Himachal assembly passes bill to deny pensions for defector MLAs
articles.summary: The Himachal Pradesh assembly passed a bill that bars MLAs disqualified under the anti-defection law from receiving pensions. The legislation, which applies to current and future members, was supported by the treasury benches but criticized by the opposition as a 'retrospective' measure targeting specific political rivals.

articles.headline: LDF manifesto for Kerala elections focuses on jobs and social security
articles.summary: The Left Democratic Front in Kerala released its assembly election manifesto, promising a ₹3,000 monthly pension for all, 20 lakh new jobs, and a 'no-homeless' state initiative. The document highlights the state's achievements in education and health while proposing massive infrastructure development under a 'New Kerala' plan.

articles.headline: Shamanur family maintains political influence in Davangere South bypolls
articles.summary: The Shamanur family continues to hold significant political sway in Davangere, with the third generation aiming to retain their seat in the South Assembly constituency. The family's legacy, combined with strong communal ties and philanthropic work, makes them a major factor in the region's politics. As bypolls approach, the influence of the 'Manchester of Karnataka' remains central.

articles.headline: BJP leader Yatnal to campaign for party nominee in Bagalkot
articles.summary: Expelled BJP leader Basanagouda R. Patil Yatnal announced he will campaign for the party's nominee, Veeranna Charantimath, in the Bagalkot bypoll to uphold the cause of Hindutva. Despite his expulsion, Yatnal stated he remains a committed party worker and will support candidates to ensure BJP victories, while expressing his belief that he will eventually return to the party.

articles.headline: LDF and UDF release manifestos for 2026 Kerala Assembly elections
articles.summary: Kerala's rival political fronts, LDF and UDF, have released their manifestos for the 2026 Assembly elections. Both promise significant welfare schemes, including pensions, health insurance, and metro rail projects for Thiruvananthapuram and Kozhikode. Key differences include UDF’s promise of free travel for women in state buses and LDF’s focus on absolute poverty eradication.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu CM Stalin terms FCRA amendment attack on minorities
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin criticized the Union government's proposed amendments to the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act, describing them as a direct attack on minority institutions. He alleged that the move aims to choke funding for NGOs and organizations working with underprivileged communities. Stalin urged for the immediate withdrawal of the proposed changes.

articles.headline: Actor Vijay files nomination for Tiruchi East by-election
articles.summary: Actor-turned-politician C. Joseph Vijay filed his nomination for the Tiruchi East Assembly by-election, his second contest. He criticized both the Union and State governments for the ongoing LPG shortage and alleged corruption in government departments. Vijay's entry into the poll fray has sparked significant interest, as his party seeks to establish itself as a major political force.

articles.headline: Union government confirms extra sittings to debate Women’s Reservation Act
articles.summary: The Union government announced that the Budget Session of Parliament will be extended by two days for sittings on April 19 and 20. The extra time is intended to debate and pass amendments to the Women's Reservation Act, which seeks to provide 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies. The Opposition has criticized the last-minute change to the schedule.

articles.headline: CAPF Bill passed in Lok Sabha amid walkout by Opposition
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha passed the Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) Bill, 2026, aimed at improving the promotional prospects and benefits for paramilitary personnel. The Bill was passed through a voice vote despite a walkout by Opposition members, who demanded that it be referred to a Joint Parliamentary Committee. Home Minister Amit Shah stated the legislation would boost the morale of the forces.

articles.headline: Amit Shah says Bengal ‘poribortan’ shortcut lies through Bhabanipur seat
articles.summary: During a campaign rally, Union Home Minister Amit Shah stated that a victory in the Bhabanipur Assembly constituency would be the quickest way to bring political change to West Bengal. Shah accompanied BJP candidate Suvendu Adhikari for the filing of his nomination. The contest in Bhabanipur is high-profile, as it has been a stronghold for Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee.

articles.headline: Congress promises ST status for six communities in Assam manifesto
articles.summary: In its manifesto for the upcoming Assam Assembly elections, the Congress party has promised to grant Scheduled Tribe status to six communities: Adivasis, Chutias, Koch-Rajbongshis, Mataks, Morans, and Tai-Ahoms. Other key promises include a Zubeen Garg Fellowship for the youth and an Anti-Corruption Commission. Party leaders emphasized the focus on social justice and cultural preservation.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi calls Assam CM Himanta Sarma most corrupt in nation
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi launched a sharp attack on Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma, calling him the 'most corrupt' CM in the country. Addressing election rallies, Gandhi alleged that the state's resources were being handed over to private players close to the BJP leadership. He also criticized the Union government's policies, claiming they favor large corporate entities.

articles.headline: IMD forecasts early monsoon arrival in Kerala by May 31
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) announced that the Southwest Monsoon is expected to hit the Kerala coast by May 31. Favorable conditions are developing in the South Andaman Sea and Bay of Bengal. A timely and normal monsoon is critical for India's agricultural output and inflation management, especially following a series of intense heatwaves in North and Central India.

articles.headline: India Implements E20 Fuel Blending Amid Low Vehicle Readiness
articles.summary: Petrol pumps across India began dispensing E20 fuel (20% ethanol blend) on April 1 to reduce crude import dependence. However, data from the Vahan dashboard shows that fewer than 30% of petrol vehicles registered in 2025 are E20-compliant. Overall, compliant vehicles account for just 3% of the total passenger vehicle and two-wheeler fleet currently on Indian roads.

articles.headline: Supreme Court stays Rajasthan notification de-notifying sanctuary land for mining
articles.summary: The Supreme Court criticized Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, and Uttar Pradesh for failing to prevent illegal sand mining in the National Chambal Sanctuary. The court stayed a Rajasthan government notification that de-notified sanctuary land, terming the administrative failure a "sad state of affairs." The bench emphasized the threat to endangered species like the Gharial and Gangetic dolphin.

articles.headline: Ten-year scientific plan unveiled to restore Delhi's degraded forest stock
articles.summary: The Forest Research Institute has prepared a ten-year blueprint for the Delhi forest department to remove invasive species like vilayati kikar, eucalyptus, and subabul. These species currently account for 40% of the city's forest stock. The plan proposes a phased removal and replanting with native flora, alongside a controversial proposal to use biological control agents like beetles.

articles.headline: National Green Tribunal dismisses challenge to Musi River rejuvenation project
articles.summary: The southern bench of the NGT dismissed a petition by BRS leader P. Kartik Reddy against Telangana’s Musi Riverfront development project. The tribunal ruled that the issues raised were already being addressed through the environmental assessment process. The state government maintained that all activities are being conducted in compliance with environmental terms of reference.

articles.headline: Nearly half of Central Vista transplanted trees have perished
articles.summary: The Union government informed the Lok Sabha that 1,545 out of 3,609 trees transplanted for the Central Vista project have died, representing a 43% failure rate. Additionally, 24,450 trees were planted as compensatory forestation. The government has spent ₹5.29 crore on these activities over three years, with the highest number of transplants occurring at the Common Central Secretariat sites.

articles.headline: India became world's third-largest renewable energy market in 2025
articles.summary: India emerged as the third-largest renewable energy market globally in 2025, following China and the United States. According to IRENA's annual report, the country added 45 Gw of renewable capacity during the year. The momentum was driven by rapid expansion in solar and wind projects, even as the West Asia crisis highlighted the volatility of fossil fuel prices.

articles.headline: Kerala on Red Alert as Heavy Pre-Monsoon Rains Cause Flooding
articles.summary: The IMD has issued a red alert for several districts in Kerala as heavy pre-monsoon rains continue to lash the state. The downpour has led to flooding in urban centers and a rise in water levels in major rivers. Disaster management teams are on high alert, and the state government has closed schools in several affected areas. The monsoon is expected to arrive earlier than usual this year.

articles.headline: Govt mandates 40% recycled content in food-grade PET packaging
articles.summary: The Indian government has mandated that food-grade PET packaging must contain at least 40% recycled material starting in April 2025. This requirement will increase gradually each year as part of an effort to reduce plastic waste and promote a circular economy. The policy aims to encourage the development of a robust recycling ecosystem for plastic packaging in the country.

articles.headline: IAF loses 444 acres of land due to forest clearance delays
articles.summary: The Indian Air Force has lost 444 acres of land in Bengaluru's Jalahalli after the state forest department reclaimed it due to failure to obtain central forest clearance. The land, which was in IAF possession since WWII, was classified as 'state forest' following a court order, hindering military expansion plans.

articles.headline: GBA directs corporations to prepare for pre-monsoon flooding
articles.summary: The Greater Bengaluru Authority has instructed five city corporations to undertake comprehensive preparedness measures ahead of the pre-monsoon season. Officials have identified 49 flood-prone locations that require immediate attention. Tasks include desilting storm water drains and roadside drains. Chief Commissioner M. Maheshwar Rao emphasized that all precautions must be completed before the rain.

articles.headline: Google begins local manufacturing of Pixel 8 smartphones in India
articles.summary: Google has officially started manufacturing its Pixel 8 smartphone series in India, aligning with the government's 'Make in India' initiative. The move aims to diversify Google's supply chain away from China and tap into the growing Indian premium smartphone market. Local production is expected to reduce logistics costs and potentially lower prices for domestic consumers over time.

articles.headline: Microsoft launches AI-powered Surface PCs to compete with Apple
articles.summary: Microsoft has unveiled a new category of 'Copilot+' PCs, including revamped Surface Pro and Laptop models featuring dedicated AI hardware. These devices use Qualcomm's ARM-based chips to run generative AI tasks locally on the device rather than the cloud. Microsoft claims the new laptops are significantly faster and offer better battery life than the latest MacBook Air models.

articles.headline: UPI Monthly Transactions Hit Record 22.64 Billion Volume in March
articles.summary: Unified Payments Interface (UPI) transactions reached a new milestone of 22.64 billion in volume terms this March, growing 24% year-on-year. The surge was driven by festive spending and financial year-end activities. The Department of Financial Services credited the growth to India's rapid embrace of digital payments for transforming daily transactions.

articles.headline: AI design startup Noon raises $44 million in stealth funding
articles.summary: AI-native product design tool Noon has emerged from stealth with $44 million in funding, the largest such round for a design-tech startup. Backers include Chemistry, First Round Capital, and leaders from OpenAI, Microsoft AI, and Stripe. Founded by Aditya Bandi and Kushagra Sinha, the company aims to innovate in the AI-driven design space.

articles.headline: Microsoft plans to develop frontier AI models to rival OpenAI
articles.summary: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman announced the company aims to develop large, cutting-edge AI models by 2027. The push is part of an internal strategy to build alternatives to high-power tools from OpenAI and Anthropic. Microsoft has begun assembling massive computing power using Nvidia GB200 chips to create models capable of generating text, images, and audio.

articles.headline: Microsoft Unveils Copilot+ PCs Featuring Integrated Artificial Intelligence
articles.summary: Microsoft has launched a new category of 'Copilot+' PCs designed to run advanced AI tasks directly on device hardware. These PCs feature a new chip architecture capable of 40 trillion operations per second. A key feature, 'Recall,' allows users to search everything they have viewed on their screens. This move marks a significant shift in the PC market toward AI-integrated local computing.

articles.headline: Bharti Airtel is Now No 2 Globally by Customers
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel has reached the milestone of 650 million customers, making it the second-largest mobile operator in the world by subscriber base. The company's operations span across India and 14 African nations. The growth is fueled by massive 5G expansion and a significant increase in high-speed data users, particularly in the Indian market.

articles.headline: IT Sector Gains 60 bps from Rupee Depreciation
articles.summary: Indian IT service companies are expected to see a margin boost of 10-60 basis points in the fourth quarter due to the rupee's sharp fall against the US dollar. Major firms like Infosys, TCS, and Wipro derive over 60% of their revenue from international markets. However, analysts warn that these gains may be partially offset by high wage costs and large-scale deal ramp-ups.

articles.headline: Lenders Wary of Rising Inflationary Risks in Fintech
articles.summary: Fintech lenders are becoming cautious as rising inflation and geopolitical tensions threaten to impact repayment capacities. Industry experts suggest that higher interest rates could lead to increased credit stress in the unsecured lending segment. Several firms are now focusing on improving their credit models and reducing exposure to high-risk consumer loans.

articles.headline: Washington Sees Trade Barrier in India IT Content Rules
articles.summary: The United States has flagged India’s information technology rules and data privacy regulations as potential trade barriers. The US Trade Representative (USTR) noted that content takedown requirements and data localization laws could impact the operations of American tech firms. The report urged India to adopt more flexible and transparent regulatory frameworks.

articles.headline: Claude AI Code Leak Reveals New Secret Features
articles.summary: A recent code leak from Anthropic’s Claude AI has revealed several unreleased features, including advanced multi-modal capabilities and a new image generation tool. The leak occurred due to a configuration error during a routine software update. Anthropic has since addressed the security vulnerability and stated that no user data was compromised in the incident.

articles.headline: LatentView Analytics invests $3 million in Healtheon AI
articles.summary: LatentView Analytics has invested $3 million in Healtheon AI to develop advanced AI solutions for the healthcare industry. The partnership will focus on improving healthcare revenue cycle management through the use of AI and data analytics. This investment is part of LatentView's strategy to expand its presence in high-growth sectors like healthcare and technology.

articles.headline: Enterprise technology spending in India remains steady despite uncertainty
articles.summary: Salesforce India CEO Arundhati Bhattacharya states that technology spending by Indian firms remains steady despite global economic uncertainty. Companies are increasingly investing in AI and automation to improve efficiency and competitiveness. The focus is on using these technologies to solve real-world business challenges and drive digital transformation across various sectors.

articles.headline: GE Aerospace delivers sixth engine for India’s Tejas Mk1A jets
articles.summary: GE Aerospace has delivered the sixth F404-IN20 engine to Hindustan Aeronautics Ltd. for the Tejas Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Mk1A program. The delivery is part of a larger contract to supply 99 engines. The new Tejas variant features several improvements over its predecessor, and the timely supply of engines is critical for the Indian Air Force's fleet modernization plans.

articles.headline: NTA considers re-exam for NEET-UG candidates over technical time loss
articles.summary: The National Testing Agency is evaluating a re-examination for nearly 1,500 NEET-UG candidates who lost time due to administrative delays at specific centers. While grace marks were initially awarded, the agency has formed a committee to review the fairness of the results following student protests. A final decision is expected before the counseling process begins in June.

articles.headline: CBSE rolls out major reforms in secondary school curriculum and exams
articles.summary: The CBSE has announced a phased implementation of the three-language formula and a two-level system for mathematics and science starting in the 2026-27 session. By 2030-31, the model will be fully operational up to Class 10. The reforms, aligned with the National Education Policy 2020, also introduce compulsory assessments for Artificial Intelligence and Computational Thinking by 2029.

articles.headline: Indian student enrollment in U.S. institutions drops nearly 7%
articles.summary: The number of Indian students enrolled in educational programs in the United States dropped 6.9% year-on-year to 352,644 as of February 2026. The Ministry of External Affairs shared data with the Rajya Sabha showing a decline from the previous year's figure of 378,787. The decline spans various programs from primary schooling to university courses.

articles.headline: CBSE introduces new curriculum with artificial intelligence and third language
articles.summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has released a new curriculum that includes a compulsory artificial intelligence (AI) and computational thinking course starting from Class 9. The overhaul also mandates a third language from Class 6 and makes vocational education compulsory. The revised curriculum will be rolled out gradually over the next few academic sessions.

articles.headline: UGC Streamlines PhD Admissions Using National Eligibility Test Scores
articles.summary: The University Grants Commission has announced that NET scores will now be used for PhD admissions starting from the 2024-25 session. This decision aims to replace multiple university entrance exams with a single national standard. Candidates will be categorized for JRF, assistant professorship, and PhD eligibility based on their performance, simplifying the process for thousands of students.

articles.headline: IT sector no longer India's primary job hub, says Xpheno chief
articles.summary: In an interview, Kamal Karanth of Xpheno discusses the slowing growth in hiring within India's IT sector. Companies are shifting towards a more cautious hiring model, with a focus on specialized skills and global capability centers. Despite the slowdown, there remains a demand for talent in emerging areas like AI and cloud computing.

articles.headline: Indian student enrolment in US universities drops by 6.9%
articles.summary: Enrolment of Indian students in US universities saw a decline of 6.9% in the current academic year. Factors like high visa rejection rates and long wait times for appointments were cited as primary reasons for the drop. The trend was discussed in Parliament, where officials noted that students are increasingly looking at other international destinations for higher education.

articles.headline: Innovation Conclave showcases student technology from tier-2 cities
articles.summary: The New Age Innovation Network (NAIN) conclave in Bengaluru highlighted technological solutions developed by students from smaller towns in Karnataka. Projects included an AI-based system for Kabaddi refereeing and sustainable agricultural tools. The NAIN program aims to nurture entrepreneurship in tier-2 and tier-3 cities, providing funding up to ₹5 lakh per student project.

articles.headline: Flutter in Karnataka over grading third language in SSLC
articles.summary: The Karnataka government's decision to award grades instead of marks for the third language paper in SSLC exams has sparked debate. The move is seen by some as a step toward a two-language policy, while others argue it reduces the importance of learning regional languages like Hindi. Data shows that 93% of students chose Hindi as their third language in the 2024-25 academic year.

articles.headline: Severe heatwave alert issued for Delhi and neighboring northern states
articles.summary: The IMD has issued a red alert as a severe heatwave continues to sweep across North India, with temperatures crossing 47 degrees Celsius in parts of Rajasthan and Haryana. The heat has pushed power demand to record highs in the national capital. Authorities have implemented heat action plans, advising citizens to avoid outdoor activities and ensuring water supply in affected areas.

articles.headline: Private hospital in Ayodhya sealed over medical negligence allegations
articles.summary: Health authorities sealed a private hospital in Ayodhya after a woman and her newborn died allegedly due to negligence by untrained staff. The family claimed the procedure was conducted by staff guided by an absent doctor over a mobile phone. An inspection revealed no qualified medical staff present, leading to the hospital's closure and a formal investigation.

articles.headline: Health Ministry Issues Advisory as Heatwave Conditions Gripe North India
articles.summary: The Health Ministry has issued a heatwave advisory as temperatures soared above 45 degrees Celsius in several parts of North and Central India. The advisory mandates states to ensure hospitals are equipped with IV fluids, ORS, and cooling equipment. Citizens are urged to stay hydrated and avoid outdoor exposure during peak afternoon hours to prevent heatstroke and related illnesses.

articles.headline: CCTV Mandatory for Medical Stores to Curb Misuse
articles.summary: The Drug Consultative Committee (DCC) has proposed making CCTV cameras mandatory at all retail pharmacies in India to monitor the sale of habit-forming drugs. The move aims to ensure that medicines are dispensed only against valid prescriptions. Pharmacy owners will be required to store footage for at least one year and provide access to regulatory authorities upon request.

articles.headline: Allied healthcare needs attention to address massive shortage
articles.summary: India faces a severe shortage of allied health professionals (AHPs), with a gap of over eight million workers. Standardised education, a national registry, and proper career pathways are needed to integrate these professionals effectively into the healthcare system. The National Commission for Allied and Healthcare Professions Act aims to address these issues and improve patient outcomes.

articles.headline: Experts call for nationwide ban on herbicide Paraquat after Telangana ban
articles.summary: Health and agriculture experts are calling for a nationwide ban on the herbicide Paraquat following its ban in Telangana. They cited its high toxicity and the risk it poses to farmers and the environment as reasons for the demand. Paraquat is already banned in over 70 countries, and experts are urging the Indian government to take similar action to protect public health.

articles.headline: Health mix manufacturing racket uncovered by police in Tumakuru
articles.summary: Tumakuru police arrested a businessman for allegedly manufacturing and selling a duplicate health powder under the brand name 'Jeeni Slim Powder' without legal approvals. The accused was operating from an unauthorized facility and selling products online. Authorities seized large quantities of the counterfeit product and related manufacturing materials during the raid on the premises.

articles.headline: Anaemia remains a major challenge for women’s health in Karnataka
articles.summary: Over 67% of pregnant women in Karnataka are affected by anaemia, according to state health officials. At a seminar hosted by RGUHS, the Chief Secretary of Karnataka emphasized the need for preventive healthcare and focus on nutrition for women. Experts highlighted that while medical reasons exist, social factors and lack of awareness also contribute significantly to high anaemia rates.

articles.headline: Diagnostics hub planned at Victoria Hospital for better public access
articles.summary: The Karnataka Health Department has proposed establishing a centralized diagnostics hub at Victoria Hospital in Bengaluru. The hub aims to provide high-end diagnostic tests that are currently unavailable in many government hospitals, forcing patients to seek private labs. The facility will be linked to several major city hospitals, with plans for home sample collection for elderly and ill patients.

articles.headline: AI-based system to monitor quality in government dialysis units
articles.summary: An AI-enabled smart monitoring system has been introduced in government-run dialysis centers in Karnataka under a public-private partnership model. The platform tracks dialysis parameters in real-time to improve clinical oversight and patient safety. Health Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao stated the system achieves 85% accuracy in monitoring and helps ensure standardized high-quality care.

articles.headline: Securing health insurance for organ transplantations remains a major hurdle
articles.summary: Patients in India are facing a widening gap between the need for organ transplants and the availability of financial protection through insurance. Post-transplant care, particularly the high cost of immunosuppressant drugs, is often not covered by private or government insurance schemes. Experts call for a unified, state-supported model to ensure long-term care for recipients and donors.

articles.headline: CAG Flags Potential ₹74,766 Crore Tax Gap in Bank Audits
articles.summary: The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) has identified potential tax implications of ₹74,766.39 crore due to gaps in compliance and reporting by 17 banks and NBFCs. The performance audit highlighted systemic issues and internal control deficiencies regarding exemptions and deductions. The finance ministry has accepted findings in several cases, with some recoveries already made.

articles.headline: Supreme Court deprecates West Bengal administration over judicial hostage crisis
articles.summary: The Supreme Court took suo motu cognizance of the eight-hour hostage-taking of seven judicial officers in Malda, West Bengal. Describing the incident as a "deplorable" attempt to browbeat the judiciary, the bench directed the Election Commission to deploy central forces. The court also ordered the CBI or NIA to investigate the administrative failure during electoral roll revisions.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court orders removal of vulgar song by Honey Singh
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has directed the immediate takedown of a two-decade-old song from the album Volume 1 by artists Honey Singh and Badshah. The court described the lyrics as "grossly vulgar" and "derogatory to women." The ruling came during a hearing concerning the impact of such content on public morality and the dignity of women in the digital space.

articles.headline: Rajasthan High Court deletes accidental criticisms of transgender rights amendment
articles.summary: A division bench of the Rajasthan High Court ordered the removal of three paragraphs from a previous verdict that had criticized amendments to the transgender rights act. The court clarified that the text in the epilogue was included by mistake and was not intended to be part of the final judgment. The bench maintained the original directions regarding reservation for transgender persons.

articles.headline: Shivamogga hotel owner arrested for murder of head cook
articles.summary: Police in Shivamogga arrested a hotel owner, Imran Khan, for allegedly killing his head cook, Tinku Sharma, over a financial dispute. The victim was suspected of stealing ₹10,000 from the hotel cash box. The accused allegedly assaulted the victim with a snooker stick and was caught while attempting to transport the body in a car.

articles.headline: ED conducts multi-city raids on I-PAC offices in coal scam
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate carried out raids at I-PAC offices in Delhi, Bengaluru, and Hyderabad as part of a money laundering probe into the West Bengal coal smuggling case. The searches included the premises of I-PAC director Rishi Raj Singh. This follows earlier searches at the firm’s Kolkata office. I-PAC has not yet issued a formal comment on the ongoing investigation.

articles.headline: Mallikarjun Kharge tells court political criticism does not constitute defamation
articles.summary: Congress President Mallikarjun Kharge told a Delhi court that a petition seeking an FIR against him for remarks about PM Modi and the RSS is an abuse of law. He argued that mere criticism of political ideologies should not be categorized as hate speech. The court was hearing a revision plea by a lawyer who claimed Kharge’s comments during a Karnataka rally were defamatory.

articles.headline: Supreme Court directs National Insurance Company CMD be named as accused
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ordered that the chairman and managing director of the state-run National Insurance Company (NIC) be named as an accused in a criminal case. The matter involves the alleged use of a forged insurance policy in a motor-accident claim. The court also ordered the formation of a Special Investigation Team (SIT) to probe the insurer's failure to initiate proceedings.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court reserves order in ANI-OpenAI copyright dispute
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has reserved its order on an interim application by Asian News International (ANI) seeking to stop OpenAI from using its news content to train ChatGPT. This is considered India's first major legal test regarding Copyright Act applications for AI training. OpenAI has denied infringement, claiming it relies on publicly available information.

articles.headline: U.S. Department of Justice to appeal block on Anthropic AI ban
articles.summary: The Trump administration will appeal a federal court order that blocked its ban on government use of Anthropic PBC’s AI technology. The Department of Justice filed a notice challenging a judge's ruling that had paused the ban while a legal fight continued. Anthropic is suing the government over claims that its technology poses a threat to the U.S. supply chain.

articles.headline: Sebi proposes reintroducing open-market share buybacks
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) has proposed to reintroduce the open-market share buyback route. The mechanism was previously phased out due to tax arbitrage concerns, but new regulations taxing buyback proceeds in the hands of shareholders have changed the landscape. The move follows requests from industry bodies arguing the route is operationally efficient.

articles.headline: Supreme Court warns Sebi regarding proceedings against Sandesara brothers
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has told market regulator Sebi that all proceedings against Sterling Biotech and its promoters, Chetan and Nitin Sandesara, must be quashed if they deposit Rs 5,100 crore in the court registry. The bench warned that it would pass a detailed order if the regulator failed to comply with the court's earlier directions from November last year.

articles.headline: Pune Porsche Crash: Two Doctors Arrested for Swapping Blood Samples
articles.summary: Pune police arrested two doctors from Sassoon General Hospital for allegedly replacing the minor driver's blood sample with another person's to hide alcohol consumption. Dr. Ajay Taware and Dr. Shrihari Halnor were detained after investigators linked them to the teen's father. This development has added fresh charges of evidence tampering and conspiracy against the doctors and the family.

articles.headline: Calcutta High Court Cancels All OBC Certificates Issued Since 2011
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court cancelled all OBC certificates issued in West Bengal after 2011, affecting nearly 5 lakh individuals. The court ruled that the inclusion of 77 classes, mostly Muslim groups, was arbitrary and illegal. While the order protects those who have already secured jobs, it halts future reservations for these groups. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has vowed to challenge the ruling.

articles.headline: Court Sends Bibhav Kumar to Judicial Custody in Maliwal Case
articles.summary: A Delhi court sent Bibhav Kumar, personal aide to CM Arvind Kejriwal, to judicial custody until May 28 in connection with the assault on AAP MP Swati Maliwal. The police informed the court that Kumar was not cooperating with the investigation and had refused to share his phone password. Maliwal has accused Kumar of brutally assaulting her at the Chief Minister's residence on May 13.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank Penalisies 12 Execs for AT1 Bond Mis-selling
articles.summary: HDFC Bank has taken action against 12 senior and mid-level executives following a Dubai regulator's probe into the mis-selling of Additional Tier-1 (AT1) bonds. The penalties include the cancellation of increments and stock options. The Dubai Financial Services Authority found that the bank's branch had failed to report incorrect practices for five years.

articles.headline: Jane Street Tax Benefit Opposed by IT Department
articles.summary: The Indian Income Tax department has opposed the granting of tax treaty benefits to the Singapore arm of Jane Street, a major global trading firm. Authorities claim that the entity does not meet the 'Principal Purpose Test' required under the India-Singapore tax treaty. The case could set a significant precedent for how derivative profits of foreign portfolio investors are taxed in India.

articles.headline: New Audit Regime to be Notified Soon by NFRA
articles.summary: The National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) is set to notify a new set of auditing standards aligned with global norms to improve corporate governance. The move follows concerns over the quality of audits in several major Indian companies. The new standards will require auditors to provide more detailed reporting on financial risks and internal controls.

articles.headline: High Court urges sensitive handling of cases involving teen relationships
articles.summary: The Uttarakhand High Court advised the police and judiciary to consider the consensual nature of relationships in cases involving teenagers before filing criminal charges. The court noted that 'Romeo-Juliet' style romances often lead to unnecessary legal trauma for young couples and their families.

articles.headline: Gherao of judicial officers in Bengal a challenge to SC: CJI
articles.summary: Chief Justice Surya Kant termed the gherao of seven judicial officers in Malda, West Bengal, a calculated move to demoralize the judiciary. The Supreme Court described the incident as a complete failure of civil and police administration. The court directed the Election Commission and national agencies to probe the matter and ensure the safety of officers involved in intensive voter roll revision.

articles.headline: Governor bound by Cabinet’s advice on remission rules Madras High Court
articles.summary: A Full Bench of the Madras High Court ruled that the Governor is bound by the advice of the Council of Ministers when exercising powers under Article 161 of the Constitution regarding the remission of convicts. The court clarified that the Governor cannot exercise individual discretion to take a different view from the Cabinet, resolving conflicting decisions from previous Division Benches.

articles.headline: Woman and infant son found dead in Bengaluru home
articles.summary: A 29-year-old woman and her 11-month-old son were found dead under suspicious circumstances in their residence in Chandra Layout, Bengaluru. The bodies were discovered by the woman's husband upon his return from work. Police have registered a case of unnatural death and are investigating the cause, including the possibility of a suicide-murder scenario, pending post-mortem results.

articles.headline: FIR registered over illegal encroachment of police land in Malleswaram
articles.summary: Malleswaram police have registered an FIR regarding the alleged illegal grab of land belonging to police quarters by a woman and her associates. The property, valued at ₹80 crore, was reportedly transferred through fraudulent property records and e-Khata. The complaint alleges that the group threatened residents and claimed ownership based on a fake agreement from over a century ago.

articles.headline: Man held for allegedly murdering wife over financial dispute
articles.summary: Peenya police arrested a businessman for allegedly strangling his wife to death following a domestic dispute over financial issues. The incident occurred at their residence on Thursday afternoon. Preliminary investigations revealed that the couple had frequent disagreements over money. Police are continuing their probe to verify the sequence of events leading to the fatal assault.

articles.headline: Legislation for internal quota to Scheduled Castes put on hold
articles.summary: Implementation of the Karnataka Scheduled Castes (Sub-classification) Bill, which provides for internal reservation, has been delayed despite receiving the Governor's assent. The government is reportedly reviewing the legal implications and awaiting a consensus on the reservation schedule. Currently, legal challenges regarding reservation quotas remain pending in the Karnataka High Court.

articles.headline: High Court declines to quash FIRs in multi-crore loan fraud case
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court refused to quash 11 First Information Reports against a Mangaluru-based businessman and his associates for allegedly cheating people of ₹40 crore under the guise of loan arrangements. The court rejected the argument that the disputes were purely civil, stating that the existence of civil remedies does not bar criminal prosecution when allegations of fraud are present.

articles.headline: ‘Amaravati Bill’ for Andhra Pradesh capital gets Rajya Sabha approval
articles.summary: The Rajya Sabha has passed the Andhra Pradesh Re-organisation (Amendment) Bill, clearing the way for Amaravati to be recognized as the capital city of the state. The move follows long-standing political and legal debates over the capital's location. Most political parties supported the Bill, which emphasizes building a sustainable capital with modern infrastructure for the state.

articles.headline: Supreme Court clarifies ECI’s powers over senior officer transfers
articles.summary: The Supreme Court provided clarifications on the Election Commission of India’s powers to transfer senior officers in election-bound states. The court noted that while the ECI has the authority to ensure free and fair polls, this power must be exercised with transparency and in line with constitutional provisions. The ruling comes after several high-level transfers in West Bengal and Assam.

articles.headline: Supreme Court slams sand mining mafia as ‘modern dacoits of Chambal’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court expressed grave concern over rampant illegal sand mining in the Chambal region, describing the mining mafia as 'modern dacoits' better armed than the police. The court stayed a notification that altered the boundaries of a wildlife sanctuary to facilitate mining, noting that it had been issued without following environmental laws and could devastate the local ecosystem.

articles.headline: Maldives Seeks Emergency Fuel Supply From India Amid Iran Conflict
articles.summary: The Maldives has approached India for both short-term and long-term supplies of petroleum products as the West Asia war threatens energy security. India’s foreign ministry is examining the request based on domestic availability. This diplomatic outreach follows severe disruptions to regional energy corridors and Qatar's warning of prolonged repairs to its gas liquefaction plants.

articles.headline: Trump Declares U.S. Military Objectives in Iran Nearly Complete
articles.summary: In a 20-minute White House address, President Trump claimed that U.S. military operations against the Iranian regime would be finished "very shortly." He insisted that the Strait of Hormuz would reopen naturally once the conflict ends and pledged to hit Iran hard if they attempt to recover enriched uranium. Markets reacted with volatility as the speech lacked a specific timeline for peace.

articles.headline: UAE Calls for UN Intervention to Forcibly Reopen Hormuz
articles.summary: The United Arab Emirates has petitioned the UN Security Council to authorize "immediate action" under Chapter Seven to restore free navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. The request includes the potential use of military force or blockades to end Iranian control over the vital energy corridor. The move signals increasing pressure from Gulf nations to secure global oil shipments.

articles.headline: China Builds Massive Military Base at Antelope Reef
articles.summary: Satellite imagery reveals that China has resumed island-building in the South China Sea, creating a 1,490-acre base at Antelope Reef in the Paracels. The facility includes jetties, a helipad, and missile installations. Analysts believe the expansion strengthens Beijing's control over Taiwan-bound thoroughfares while global attention is focused on conflicts in West Asia.

articles.headline: Trump pledges continued conflict in Iran as Gulf strikes persist
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump announced plans to extend military operations in Iran for several weeks, targeting electric plants if demands aren't met. Despite claims of reduced threats, Iran continues missile attacks on Israel and Gulf states while seeking to control the Strait of Hormuz. Global markets reacted with volatility as oil prices remained elevated and stock prices fell.

articles.headline: India joins UK-led meeting to seek diplomatic solution for Hormuz
articles.summary: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri represented India in a virtual meeting chaired by the UK to explore ways to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. India emphasized the importance of unimpeded transit through international waterways. The meeting included representatives from France, Germany, Italy, Canada, and the UAE to address the global energy supply disruptions.

articles.headline: India condemns attacks on UNIFIL peacekeepers in Lebanon
articles.summary: India has strongly condemned recent attacks on the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) that resulted in the deaths of three Indonesian peacekeepers. External Affairs Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal called for the safety and inviolability of UN missions. India, a major troop contributor to UNIFIL, sought accountability for crimes against peacekeepers amid an Israeli ground offensive.

articles.headline: Argentina expels Iranian diplomat following terrorist designation of IRGC
articles.summary: The government of President Javier Milei has expelled Iran's charge d'affaires from Argentina. The move follows Argentina's designation of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps as a terrorist organization. The foreign ministry cited "false and offensive accusations" from Tehran as the reason for the expulsion, amid deepening ties between Argentina, the US, and Israel.

articles.headline: World Bank and IMF to form joint group on West Asia war
articles.summary: The heads of the IMF, World Bank, and International Energy Agency (IEA) have agreed to form a coordination group to monitor the energy and economic impacts of the West Asia war. The group will focus on data sharing regarding global energy markets, prices, and supply chain disruptions to provide coordinated support to low-income countries affected by the crisis.

articles.headline: Trump vows aggressive strikes to bring Iran back to 'Stone Age'
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump has promised intensified strikes on Iran over the next two to three weeks, aiming to destroy its energy and oil infrastructure. Trump stated that Washington would achieve its military objectives soon but warned of further escalation if Iranian leaders did not accept his terms. The address sent global oil prices higher and lowered share prices.

articles.headline: China communicates with stakeholders regarding Strait of Hormuz stability
articles.summary: China has confirmed it is in talks with all involved parties regarding the need to restore stability in the Strait of Hormuz. Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning called for an early ceasefire and peace talks. China and Pakistan have also agreed to promote a five-point proposal to guarantee the safety of navigation in the vital waterway.

articles.headline: U.S. trade deficit widens to $57.3 billion in February
articles.summary: The U.S. trade deficit in goods and services grew 4.9% to $57.3 billion in February, though the gap was smaller than economists' forecasts. Exports rose 4.2% driven by gold and natural gas, while imports increased 4.3% on higher shipments of computers and automobiles. The data reflects the ongoing impact of the administration's aggressive tariff policies.

articles.headline: Spain, Norway, and Ireland Formally Recognize Palestinian Statehood
articles.summary: In a significant diplomatic move, Spain, Norway, and Ireland announced their formal recognition of a Palestinian state to promote a two-state solution. Israel reacted sharply, recalling its ambassadors from the three nations and labeling the decision a 'reward for terrorism.' The recognition comes amid mounting international pressure for a ceasefire in the ongoing conflict in Gaza.

articles.headline: Global Leaders Attend Funeral of Iran President Raisi in Tehran
articles.summary: Thousands gathered in Tehran for the funeral of President Ebrahim Raisi and Foreign Minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian, who died in a helicopter crash. Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei led the prayers. The deaths have triggered a transition of power, with acting President Mohammad Mokhber overseeing the government until new elections are held on June 28 to choose a successor.

articles.headline: Hit Irans Electricity Plan Says Primetime Trump
articles.summary: Donald Trump has called for targeted strikes on Iran's electrical grid and energy infrastructure to pressure the country into ending the Hormuz shipping blockade. Speaking in a primetime address, he suggested that a 'crude shock' is necessary to protect global energy markets. Oil prices surged past $110 per barrel following his comments, as fears of a wider conflict escalated.

articles.headline: Attack on Bushehr Nuclear Plant Would Be War Crime
articles.summary: Iran’s representative to the International Atomic Energy Agency has warned that any military strike on the Bushehr nuclear power plant would be considered a 'war crime' under international law. The statement follows reports of planned US and Israeli operations to target Iran's nuclear facilities. Iran maintains that its nuclear program is for peaceful energy purposes only.

articles.headline: Still no endgame in sight for West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Market uncertainty remains high as Donald Trump's rhetoric and the ongoing conflict in West Asia impact global oil prices and trade. Tensions have led to rising energy costs and shipping delays, with no clear resolution in sight. Analysts suggest that the prolonged conflict could have further negative effects on global economic stability and market sentiment.

articles.headline: ECB member Gabriel Makhlouf says April rate hike possible
articles.summary: European Central Bank Governor Gabriel Makhlouf indicated that an interest rate hike in April is possible if inflation remains above the target level. While the ECB will take a data-dependent approach, high energy prices and global supply disruptions are putting upward pressure on inflation. The bank aims to bring inflation back to its 2% target in the coming months.

articles.headline: Modi and Russian Dy PM discuss deepening strategic and economic ties
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi and Russian Deputy PM Denis Manturov met in New Delhi to discuss strategic cooperation in trade, energy, and logistics. Both nations aim to strengthen their economic partnership amid ongoing global tensions. The talks focused on improving financial infrastructure and expanding trade in local currencies to facilitate smoother transactions between the two countries.

articles.headline: Trump signals end of Iran war, hints at more military action
articles.summary: President Donald Trump stated that the military conflict with Iran is 'nearly over' but warned of further escalation to ensure the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. The US is leading a 40-country coalition to secure the vital oil shipping lane, despite skepticism from European allies over the military approach.

articles.headline: Foreign Secretary represents India at global meeting on energy transit security
articles.summary: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri represented India in a US-hosted virtual meeting with over 60 countries to discuss the security of the Strait of Hormuz. India emphasized the importance of freedom of navigation and the protection of international trade routes vital for energy security amid escalating regional tensions.

articles.headline: Macron slams US strategy on Iran, calls for diplomatic solution
articles.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has criticized the U.S. approach to the Iran conflict, warning that military threats to reopen the Strait of Hormuz are unrealistic and dangerous. Macron urged for a return to diplomacy, stating that European allies should not be forced into a broader war that lacks a clear exit strategy.

articles.headline: Iran fires missiles at Israel as Trump talks of winding down war
articles.summary: Iran launched a fresh volley of missiles at Israel and Gulf Arab states, disrupting global energy markets and shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Despite the escalation, U.S. President Donald Trump claimed the threat was nearly eliminated and predicted the war would end soon. Trump suggested the U.S. could build alternative pipelines to bypass the region, as Tehran defended its strikes.

articles.headline: India leads 16-nation maritime security initiative in Indian Ocean
articles.summary: India has taken the lead in a 16-nation maritime security initiative to protect international shipping lanes in the Indian Ocean. The initiative, involving navies from various regions, focuses on addressing threats like piracy, armed robbery, and maritime terrorism. The Indian Navy has deployed ships to monitor critical waterways and ensure the free flow of trade amid regional tensions.

articles.headline: Trump issues ‘Stone Age’ bombing threat to Iran as conflict escalates
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump warned Iran that it could face 'Stone Age' destruction if it does not halt missile attacks on Israel and international shipping. Trump outlined objectives to degrade Iran's missile industry and ensure it never obtains a nuclear weapon. Meanwhile, Tehran maintains its strikes are defensive and has called on the U.S. to end its military presence in the region.

articles.headline: Aamir Khan Tests Pay-Per-View Model With Sitaare Zameen Par
articles.summary: Actor Aamir Khan’s latest film will stream on SonyLIV after an initial pay-per-view rental period on YouTube. This experimental move tests the viability of transaction video-on-demand (TVoD) in India, where audiences typically expect films bundled with subscriptions. TVoD revenues in India are projected to reach ₹740 crore by 2028 as the market evolves.

articles.headline: Thiruvananthapuram airport suspends operations for centuries-old temple ritual
articles.summary: Flight services at Thiruvananthapuram International Airport were paused for five hours to allow the bi-annual 'Painkuni Arattu' procession of Sree Padmanabhaswamy Temple to pass. This decades-old tradition involves the procession crossing the airport tarmac to reach Shankumugham beach. The idols of the deities were escorted by members of the Travancore royal family and devotees.

articles.headline: Easter long weekend triggers surge in international and domestic travel
articles.summary: The Easter long weekend has seen a 5-8% year-on-year increase in travel bookings, with hill stations like Mussoorie and international short-haul trips gaining traction. Occupancies at premium hotels and luxury villas are expected to reach 90-95%. Dynamic pricing has pushed nightly rates up by 20-30% as young couples from metro cities drive seasonal demand.

articles.headline: The Ahom Moidams Recommended for UNESCO World Heritage Status
articles.summary: The International Council on Monuments and Sites has recommended the Ahom Dynasty Moidams in Assam for inclusion in the UNESCO World Heritage list. These mound-burial systems represent unique architectural and cultural traditions of the Ahom kingdom. If approved, it will be the first cultural heritage site from Northeast India to receive this status, boosting regional tourism and preservation.

articles.headline: Couturier Tarun Tahiliani opens luxury label store in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Fashion designer Tarun Tahiliani has launched his luxury prêt label 'OTT' at the Phoenix Mall of Asia in Bengaluru. The collection features a blend of traditional Indian and modern western aesthetics, with pieces like draped saris and structured corsets. Tahiliani's new line aims to cater to the city's evolving fashion sense and the growing demand for 'global Indian' attire.

articles.headline: Rick Allen of Def Leppard reflects on his drumming journey
articles.summary: Legendary drummer Rick Allen, known for his resilience after losing an arm in a car accident, shared insights into his career and technique. Speaking before a performance in India, Allen highlighted how he developed new neural pathways to play drums with both hands and feet. He emphasized the power of music as a form of escapism and its ability to connect with people globally.

articles.headline: India’s Space Startups See Surge in Demand for Surveillance Data
articles.summary: The US-Iran war has triggered a global surge in demand for space-based surveillance, benefiting Indian startups like Pixxel, Digantara, and GalaxEye. Pixxel recently signed a deal with Saudi Arabia’s Neo Space Group for high-resolution Earth imagery. Space surveillance is projected to generate $8 billion in annual revenue for India by 2033 as nations seek independent data sources.

articles.headline: Nasa's Artemis II mission launches crew for 10-day moon journey
articles.summary: Nasa launched its Artemis II mission on Wednesday, sending four astronauts on a high-speed flight around the moon. This marks the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years. The 10-day journey is designed to test Orion’s life-support and navigation systems. The mission includes the first woman and person of color to fly beyond low-earth orbit.

articles.headline: Artemis II mission to open $127 billion lunar economy by 2050
articles.summary: Nasa’s Artemis II mission is expected to catalyze a lunar economy worth up to $127 billion by 2050. The mission is the first crewed flight to the vicinity of the moon in 50 years. Industry experts say this will create massive business opportunities for space startups and private companies, with India aiming to land an astronaut on the moon surface by 2040.

articles.headline: Understanding Clear Air Turbulence Following Singapore Airlines Incident
articles.summary: The Explained section details 'Clear Air Turbulence' after a Singapore Airlines flight hit severe turbulence, resulting in one fatality. This phenomenon occurs when air masses at different speeds meet without visible clouds. Experts note that climate change is making jet streams more volatile, leading to more frequent and intense turbulence, which remains difficult for modern radar to detect.

articles.headline: It’s a Race to the Moon Again: Artemis II Takes Off
articles.summary: NASA has successfully launched the Artemis II mission, marking the first crewed flight to the moon's vicinity in over 50 years. The mission carries four astronauts on a 10-day journey to orbit the moon and test deep-space navigation systems. This is a critical precursor to landing the first woman and person of color on the lunar surface by 2030.

articles.headline: IFFCO projects nano nutrients to replace 10% of conventional fertilizers
articles.summary: IFFCO expects nano-fertilizers to replace 10% of conventional fertilizer use in India within the next 3-4 years. This shift is expected to reduce India's import dependency and improve soil health by providing more efficient nutrient delivery to crops. The adoption of nano-fertilizers is part of a larger effort to promote sustainable agriculture practices in the country.

articles.headline: IAF's Tejas Mk1 fleet to resume flying after safety checks
articles.summary: The Indian Air Force's fleet of Tejas Mk1 fighter jets, which was grounded after a crash in February, is set to resume operations next week. A thorough safety check and root-cause analysis have been completed by HAL and other stakeholders. The resumption of flights is seen as a positive step for the indigenous aircraft program and India's air defense capabilities.

articles.headline: Artemis II mission lifts off for first crewed Moon voyage
articles.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully launched from Cape Canaveral with a crew of four astronauts, including the first woman and first person of color to travel to the Moon's vicinity. The 10-day mission, occurring over 50 years after Apollo 17, marks a significant step toward establishing a permanent lunar presence.

articles.headline: Artemis II mission makes historic lift-off for crewed lunar flyby
articles.summary: Four astronauts launched on a journey around the moon from Kennedy Space Center aboard a NASA rocket. The Artemis II mission is the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years, marking a major milestone in human deep-space exploration. The crew includes three U.S. astronauts and one Canadian, tasked with testing systems for future landings as global competition for lunar presence intensifies.

articles.headline: ISRO invites research proposals for data from Aditya-L1 mission
articles.summary: The Indian Space Research Organisation has issued its second Announcement of Opportunity for analysis of data from the Aditya-L1 solar mission. Scientists and researchers are invited to submit proposals for analyzing the sun's activities based on observations from the spacecraft's instruments. The move aims to maximize the scientific output from India’s first dedicated solar observatory.

articles.headline: Artemis II: Human return to the moon after 50 years
articles.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission, carrying four astronauts, aims to test essential systems for deep-space exploration and lunar landing. This mission will fly around the moon before returning to Earth, preparing the way for subsequent missions to establish a base on the lunar surface. The project is critical to U.S. efforts to maintain space leadership amid growing competition from China's space program.

articles.headline: Former Maoist stronghold Puvarti sees transformation through governance and schools
articles.summary: Puvarti village, once the headquarters of Maoist commander Madvi Hidma, is undergoing a shift toward stability following the establishment of a security camp. The village now has its first primary school, ration shop, and health services under the Niyad Nellanar Yojana. Officials report that basic infrastructure, including roads and electricity, is finally reaching this remote region.

articles.headline: Family of veterinarian killed by hippopotamus alleges negligence in Shivamogga
articles.summary: The family of 27-year-old veterinarian Dr. Sameeksha Reddy, who was killed in a hippopotamus attack at Shivamogga zoo, has sought a thorough investigation into the incident. They have approached the police alleging procedural negligence and lack of safety protocols. The family claims the doctor was asked to enter the enclosure at an unusual hour and that safety norms were not followed.

articles.headline: Ayurveda student dies by suicide in Chitradurga over relationship rumours
articles.summary: A 23-year-old BAMS student in Karnataka’s Chitradurga district died by suicide following rumors about an alleged relationship with a lecturer. A note recovered from the scene named the lecturer’s wife and several students, accusing them of harassment. Police have registered a case of abetment to suicide and are investigating the claims of mental distress and defamation.

articles.headline: Bengaluru mother dies by suicide following accidental drowning of her child
articles.summary: A 29-year-old software professional in Bengaluru died by suicide after her 11-month-old son accidentally drowned in a bucket of water. The child fell into the water while the mother was on the terrace. Overcome with guilt and shock, she took the extreme step later that day. Police have registered a case of accidental death for the child and an unnatural death report for the mother.

articles.headline: Chikkamagaluru forest official caught on video demanding bribe from farmer
articles.summary: A Deputy Range Forest Officer in Chikkamagaluru has come under fire after a video surfaced showing him demanding a bribe to assess crop damage. The official allegedly asked for "money for expenses" from a farmer whose crops were destroyed in an elephant attack. The forest department is reviewing the footage for potential disciplinary action against the officer.

articles.headline: Elderly woman murdered for jewellery in Rajasthan; legs severed by assailants
articles.summary: A 65-year-old woman was found dead in a dry pond in Rajasthan’s Pali district with her legs severed to steal silver anklets. Police suspect the attackers slit her throat before mutilating the body to remove ornaments. The victim, Pyari Devi, was discovered by locals on Wednesday night. An investigation is underway to identify the suspects involved in the brutal robbery and murder.

articles.headline: Hyderabad police seize over 400 LPG cylinders hidden in graveyard
articles.summary: Police in Hyderabad seized 414 cooking gas canisters hidden in a graveyard and arrested individuals involved in black-market sales. The raid is part of a broader crackdown on hoarding as the West Asia conflict disrupts supplies. Authorities have conducted over 2,600 raids recently to prevent illegal profiteering during the ongoing energy shortage caused by the Iran war.

articles.headline: Mother and occultist arrested for suspected human sacrifice in Jharkhand
articles.summary: Police in Jharkhand’s Hazaribagh arrested a woman and an occult practitioner for the alleged sacrificial killing of a 13-year-old girl. The mother allegedly strangled her daughter on the occultist's advice to cure her sick son. The incident occurred during Ram Navami celebrations. The Jharkhand High Court has taken suo motu cognizance of the case, seeking reports from the DGP.

articles.headline: Over 20 Indian workers died abroad daily over past five years
articles.summary: Government data shows that over 20 Indian workers died abroad every day between 2021 and 2025, totaling 37,740 fatalities. Minister of State for External Affairs Kirti Vardhan Singh informed the Rajya Sabha that most deaths occurred in Gulf nations. Annual casualties have risen steadily from 6,614 in 2022 to 7,854 in 2025.

articles.headline: Foreign Secretary Misri attends UK-led meet on Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri participated in a virtual meeting of 35 countries hosted by the UK to explore reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Misri highlighted the importance of unimpeded transit through international waterways and the impact of the crisis on India's energy security. India remains the only nation to have lost mariners in recent attacks on merchant shipping in the Gulf.

articles.headline: NHRC Notices Bengal Government Over Reports of Post-Poll Violence
articles.summary: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has issued a notice to the West Bengal government regarding reports of post-poll violence across the state. The commission has demanded a detailed report within two weeks on the steps taken to curb violence and protect citizens. The state government has maintained that the situation is stable and that allegations are politically motivated.

articles.headline: Ganderbal Killing: J&K Cops Probe Fake Encounter Allegations
articles.summary: Jammu and Kashmir police are investigating allegations of a fake encounter following the death of a youth in Ganderbal. Political leaders including Omar Abdullah and Mehbooba Mufti have called for a transparent probe into the incident. The police stated that the deceased was killed during a counter-insurgency operation, but the family claims he had no militant links.

articles.headline: Mounting Everest rescue scam: $20 million fraud under investigation
articles.summary: Nepal police are investigating an alleged $20 million insurance fraud involving trekking operators, hospitals, and pilots. Scammers allegedly forced hikers to undergo unnecessary helicopter evacuations or inflated medical bills by inducing illness symptoms, potentially damaging the reputation of Nepal's mountain tourism industry.

articles.headline: Former Infosys employees allegedly dupe firm of ₹6 crore
articles.summary: Bengaluru police have booked former senior employees of Mysore Mercantile Co Ltd and former Infosys staff for an alleged ₹6 crore fraud. The accused reportedly used forged documents and false representations of CSR projects to divert funds to unauthorized bank accounts between 2023 and 2024.

articles.headline: Nilgiri-class stealth frigate INS Taragiri to be commissioned in Visakhapatnam
articles.summary: INS Taragiri, a multi-mission stealth guided-missile frigate under Project 17 Alpha, will be commissioned into the Indian Navy today at the Eastern Naval Command dockyard. Defense Minister Rajnath Singh will preside over the ceremony. The ship is designed to provide maritime security and can protect aircraft carrier groups from various threats using its advanced electronic warfare systems.

articles.headline: Andhra CM Naidu celebrates completion of Polavaram project diaphragm wall
articles.summary: Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu termed the completion of the 1,372-metre-long diaphragm wall at the Polavaram project a major milestone. He noted that the reconstruction of the wall, which was damaged during the previous regime, cost ₹1,000 crore. The completion of this structure is expected to accelerate work on the main dam, with a goal of finishing the project by 2027.

articles.headline: Agreement signed for India's first indigenous coal gasification project
articles.summary: Bharat Coal Gasification and Chemicals (BCGCL) and Mahanadi Coalfields (MCL) have signed a deal for a Rs 25,000 crore coal-to-ammonium nitrate project in Odisha. The facility will deploy indigenously developed technology from BHEL and produce 2,000 tonnes per day. This project is part of a national push to reduce energy imports and utilize India's vast coal reserves.

articles.headline: Railways to Launch 50 New Amrit Bharat Trains for Common Passengers
articles.summary: Indian Railways plans to introduce 50 new Amrit Bharat trains following successful pilot runs. These non-AC trains are designed to provide better amenities and higher speeds for common passengers. Features include mobile charging points, improved interiors, and zero-discharge modular toilets. Production will commence at the Integral Coach Factory to meet the growing demand for fast travel.

articles.headline: Realty Market Holds Ground with Rising Local Capital
articles.summary: India's real estate sector saw $1.6 billion in institutional investment in the first quarter of 2024, with domestic investors contributing nearly 75% of the total. The office and residential segments remained the primary drivers of growth, particularly in cities like Bengaluru and Delhi-NCR. Experts suggest that strong domestic liquidity is helping the market withstand global headwinds.

articles.headline: Payment Norms for Highway Builders Relaxed by Centre
articles.summary: The Ministry of Road Transport and Highways has relaxed payment norms for road developers to help them manage cash flow issues caused by the West Asia crisis. Monthly payouts will now be provided instead of quarterly, and builders will be allowed more time to meet construction milestones. The move is designed to prevent project delays and support the infrastructure sector's growth.

articles.headline: Govt pushes PNG expansion to 30 lakh connections
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas is reviewing plans to expand piped natural gas (PNG) to 30 lakh households across high-priority districts. The initiative aims to improve energy infrastructure and provide a cleaner fuel source for domestic use. Officials are coordinating with local authorities to fast-track pipeline installation in urban and rural areas.

articles.headline: Integral Coach Factory rolls out record 3,405 coaches in FY26
articles.summary: The Integral Coach Factory (ICF) in Chennai manufactured a record 3,405 coaches in FY26, marking a 13% increase from the previous year. The achievement supports Indian Railways' efforts to modernize its fleet and improve regional connectivity. ICF's production includes various types of coaches for both passenger and express trains.

articles.headline: CapitaLand Investment outlines 23 million square feet development in India
articles.summary: CapitaLand Investment is planning a 23 million square feet development pipeline in India, focusing on sectors like logistics and data centers. The company aims to capitalize on the growing demand for industrial and digital infrastructure in the country. This expansion is part of CapitaLand's long-term strategy to grow its presence in the Indian market.

articles.headline: Bengaluru vehicle count reaches 1.2 crore with 2,100 new daily registrations
articles.summary: Bengaluru's vehicle population has hit 1.2 crore as of March 31, with an average of 2,100 new vehicles registered daily. Two-wheelers dominate the growth, accounting for 86,014 of the new vehicles added. Experts cite inadequate public transport as the primary reason for the surge, which continues to exacerbate city traffic congestion.

articles.headline: GBA may regularise 15% building plan deviations with heavy penalties
articles.summary: The Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA) has proposed regularizing building deviations from sanctioned plans by up to 15%, up from the current 5%. While the move provides relief to property owners, it includes stiff penalties and a minimum consideration of ₹50,000 per square foot to discourage unauthorized constructions.

articles.headline: Rail infrastructure upgrades for Bengaluru-Dharwad and Bengaluru-Hyderabad routes
articles.summary: The South Western Railway has received sanction for major track upgrades. The Bengaluru-Dharwad route via Yeshwantpur will see speed increases to 130kmph. Additionally, a new 5km double line between Byappanahalli and Bengaluru East has been commissioned to improve suburban train punctuality and capacity.

articles.headline: BDA floats ₹420 crore tender for Mekhri Circle four-lane flyover
articles.summary: The Bangalore Development Authority has invited tenders for a 1.6km elevated four-lane flyover at the congested Mekhri Circle. The project, estimated at ₹419.7 crore, includes down-ramps towards Yeshwantpur and Jayamahal to ease traffic flow. Construction is expected to take 18 months once started.

articles.headline: Greater Bengaluru Authority floats tender for ‘Guarantee Seva Kendras’
articles.summary: The GBA has invited tenders to establish 10 comprehensive citizen service centers across Bengaluru. These ‘Guarantee Seva Kendras’ will offer nearly 24 services, including property tax payments, trade licenses, and birth certificates, under one roof. The initiative aims to streamline public service delivery and reduce the need for citizens to visit multiple government offices for approvals.

articles.headline: Proposal to raise permissible construction deviation limit to 15%
articles.summary: The five city corporations of Bengaluru have proposed increasing the permissible deviation limit in buildings from 5% to 15%. This move, aimed at regularizing constructions that vary from sanctioned plans, seeks to resolve issues for property owners who struggle to get occupancy certificates and utility connections. The GBA described the decision as a people-friendly policy for the city.

articles.headline: Over 3 lakh Bengaluru properties may receive OC relief
articles.summary: A proposal to relax permissible building deviation limits from 5% to 15% could provide relief to over 3 lakh property owners in Bengaluru who currently lack Occupancy Certificates. The relaxation would allow these properties to secure formal utility connections like power and water. The draft rules also specify compounding fees for regularizing deviations within the new permissible limits.

articles.headline: GBA’s Seva Kendras to provide doorstep delivery of citizen services
articles.summary: The Greater Bengaluru Authority's new Guarantee Seva Kendras (GSK) will feature ‘Seva Mitras’ who will visit residents' homes to assist with applications and documentation. Each GSK will be equipped with modern digital infrastructure, offering services like Khata issuance, trade licenses, and building plan approvals. The centers will operate from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. to enhance citizen convenience.

articles.headline: BDA floats tenders for Bengaluru Master Plan 2041 update
articles.summary: The Bengaluru Development Authority has invited tenders to prepare a Revised Master Plan-2041 for its jurisdiction. The project involves creating a base map and an actual plan for approximately 450 sq. km on the city's periphery. The exercise is estimated to cost ₹8 crore and aims to provide a structured framework for the city's growth over the next two decades.

articles.headline: Bengaluru Metro extends services till 2 a.m. for IPL
articles.summary: BMRCL will extend metro train services until 2 a.m. on April 6 for the Indian Premier League match scheduled at Chinnaswamy Stadium. Last trains will depart from all terminal stations around 11 p.m. on the match day and run late into the night. Additional security and staff will be deployed at stations near the venue to manage the expected surge in commuters and ensure smooth movement.

articles.headline: APSRTC bus crashes into median in Bengaluru; no injuries
articles.summary: An Andhra Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation bus crashed into a road median near Shivananda Circle on Thursday. The driver reportedly lost control while trying to avoid hitting an autorickshaw that had swerved into the bus's path. Despite the impact, which caused significant damage to the bus's front portion, no passengers or pedestrians were injured in the accident.

articles.headline: Telangana launches Bhu Bharati portal to streamline land registrations
articles.summary: The Telangana government has launched the Bhu Bharati portal to simplify the land registration process and replace the previous Dharani system. The new portal aims to eliminate the need for frequent visits to registration offices and address issues with land records. It will be rolled out in a phased manner across the state's districts to ensure efficiency and transparency.

articles.headline: Uzbek teen Javokhir Sindarov takes lead at Candidates tournament
articles.summary: 20-year-old Javokhir Sindarov has taken the sole lead in the Candidates tournament in Cyprus after winning three out of four games. India's R Praggnanandhaa sits a point and a half behind after a bruising defeat to the Uzbek Grandmaster. In the women's section, Anna Muzychuk and Bibisara Assaubayeva lead the standings as Indian players Divya Deshmukh and Vaishali seek their first wins.

articles.headline: Sameer Rizvi guides Delhi Capitals to victory as Impact Player
