articles.summary: Sameer Rizvi played a match-winning unbeaten 70 off 47 balls to help Delhi Capitals secure a six-wicket win over Lucknow Super Giants. Coming in as an Impact Player when DC was in trouble at 26/4, Rizvi anchored the chase with five boundaries and four sixes. His performance validated the controversial rule, helping his side finish the game with 17 balls to spare.

articles.headline: Archer Prathamesh Jawkar returns to compound for Olympic dreams
articles.summary: Indian archer Prathamesh Jawkar has returned to compound archery after an eight-month stint in recurve, motivated by compound's inclusion in the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics. He recently secured an individual silver at the Asia Cup in Bangkok, marking his first medal since his return. Jawkar noted that while recurve training improved his fitness, his solid base remains in compound.

articles.headline: Shuttler Tanvi Sharma rises to world No. 34 in senior rankings
articles.summary: 17-year-old badminton player Tanvi Sharma has reached a career-best world ranking of 34 in the senior circuit. Training under South Korean coach Park Tae-sang in Guwahati, the youngster has impressed with her technical variety and wins over higher-ranked opponents. While she works on improving her stamina, she is tipped as a future star for Indian women's badminton.

articles.headline: FIGC head Gabriele Gravina resigns following Italy’s World Cup failure
articles.summary: Gabriele Gravina, the head of Italy’s football federation, resigned after the men’s national team failed to qualify for the World Cup for a third consecutive time. The decision followed a playoff loss to Bosnia and Herzegovina. The federation announced that a vote for a new president will be held on June 22 as Italian football seeks a path to recovery from another humiliation.

articles.headline: KKR Qualify for IPL Final After Eight-Wicket Win Over SRH
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders reached the IPL 2024 final with a dominant eight-wicket victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad in Qualifier 1. Mitchell Starc's fiery opening spell took three wickets, restricting SRH to 159. Venkatesh Iyer and Shreyas Iyer then struck unbeaten half-centuries to chase the target in just 13.4 overs. SRH will now play the winner of the Eliminator to stay in the tournament.

articles.headline: Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Knight Riders by 82 runs in IPL 2026
articles.summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad secured a dominant 82-run victory over Kolkata Knight Riders at Eden Gardens. Led by half-centuries from Travis Head, Heinrich Klaasen, and Nitish Kumar, SRH posted a massive total of 226/8. In response, KKR's batting collapsed under pressure from spinners and disciplined bowling by Jaydev Unadkat, who took four wickets. The win marks SRH's first points of the season.

articles.headline: India storms past Bhutan 5-0 to enter SAFF u-20 final
articles.summary: Defending champions India secured a spot in the final of the SAFF u-20 championship with a 5-0 victory over Bhutan. Rohit Singh and Thokchom scored multiple goals as India controlled the match from the start. They will now face Bangladesh in the final after they defeated Nepal 1-0 in the other semifinal. India is looking to win its fourth consecutive title in the regional tournament.

articles.headline: Aditya routs Alhawsaw to make boxing quarterfinals in 65kg category
articles.summary: National champion Aditya Pratap Yadav recorded a dominant 5-0 win over Saudi Arabia’s Mousa Alhawsaw to reach the quarterfinals of the Asian boxing championships in Ulaanbaatar. Aditya showcased superior technical skills throughout the three rounds to advance in the 65kg category. He will next face Uzbekistan’s Abdulloh Madaminov for a place in the medal rounds.

articles.headline: Vaibhav Suryavanshi: Raging debate over fast-tracking 15-year-old batter
articles.summary: A debate has emerged in the cricket world about whether 15-year-old Vaibhav Suryavanshi should be fast-tracked to the Indian white-ball team. Suryavanshi recently became the youngest player to score a half-century in IPL history, hitting 50 off 15 balls for Rajasthan Royals. While his talent is clear, experts caution against catapulting a teenager to the highest level without domestic experience.

articles.headline: India's Annual Defence Exports Hit Record ₹38,424 Crore in FY26
articles.summary: India’s defence exports surged 63% to a record ₹38,424 crore in FY26, up from ₹23,622 crore. DPSUs led growth with a 151% increase to ₹21,071 crore, while the private sector contributed ₹17,353 crore. Defense Minister Rajnath Singh attributed the rise to policy reforms and demand for hardware like BrahMos missiles and AI drones, marking a significant step toward self-reliance.

articles.headline: India Manufacturing PMI Falls to 45-Month Low in March
articles.summary: The HSBC India Manufacturing PMI dropped to 53.9 in March from 56.9 in February, its lowest level since June 2022. The slowdown was driven by softer domestic demand, West Asia war disruptions, and high input costs for fuel and steel. Despite the 45-month low, the reading remains above the 50-mark threshold, indicating continued expansion even as new orders and output growth cooled.

articles.headline: Government waives customs duty on critical petrochemical products until June
articles.summary: The Indian government has exempted 40 key petrochemical products, including Methanol, Ethylene, and Propylene, from customs duty until June 30, 2026. This CBIC move aims to stabilize supply chains and protect manufacturers in pharma and textiles from volatility caused by the West Asia conflict. The tax break is expected to result in a revenue loss of approximately ₹1,800 crore.

articles.headline: Indian rupee logs biggest single-day gain in over 12 years
articles.summary: The Indian rupee rallied 1.8% (173 paise) to settle at 93.10 per dollar, marking its best single-day gain since September 2013. The surge followed RBI measures to clamp down on speculative activities and prohibit lenders from offering certain non-deliverable derivative contracts. The recovery comes after the currency hit an intraday record low of 95.21 in the previous session.

articles.headline: RBI allows residents to exchange rupee notes at airport forex counters
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has permitted both residents and non-residents to exchange Indian rupee notes for foreign currency at authorized counters in international airport departure halls. Previously restricted, the facility is now available in designated Security Hold Areas beyond immigration desks. The move aims to facilitate easier travel for passengers leaving the country.

articles.headline: Gram Panchayats in Karnataka achieve 89% tax collection target
articles.summary: Karnataka's 5,943 gram panchayats collected a record ₹1,382.6 crore in property tax for FY 2025-26, reaching 89% of their target. Bengaluru Rural and Belagavi led the collections. Minister Priyank Kharge attributed the success to the 'Bhu Bharati' portal and increased rural industrialization, noting that 12 districts achieved over 100% of their individual revenue targets.

articles.headline: Civil aviation ministry suspends mandatory free seat allocation rule
articles.summary: The Ministry of Civil Aviation has put in abeyance its directive requiring airlines to offer at least 60% of seats without a selection fee. The suspension follows objections from the Federation of Indian Airlines and Akasa Air regarding the rule's impact on fare structures and the deregulated tariff regime. The DGCA will still enforce other measures, such as co-seating for families.

articles.headline: Sebi Proposes Reintroducing Open Market Share Buybacks via Exchanges
articles.summary: SEBI is considering reintroducing open market share buybacks through a separate window on stock exchanges, reversing a plan to phase them out by 2025. The route was previously discouraged due to tax distortions, but recent tax changes have balanced the system. The proposal aims to provide flexibility during market corrections, and public comments are invited until April 23.

articles.headline: West Asia Tensions Delay Global Capital Flows into Indian VC Funds
articles.summary: Escalating conflict in West Asia is delaying fresh capital commitments to India-focused venture capital funds as international investors adopt a wait-and-watch approach. Experts note that Gulf-based sovereign wealth funds are prioritizing domestic deployment. At least one firm reported a cancelled $3 million commitment, signaling a potential slowdown after a record $23.2 billion raised in 2025.

articles.headline: TVS Venu Group to Acquire PGIM’s Indian Mutual Fund Business
articles.summary: The Sudarshan Venu-led TVS Venu Group has agreed to acquire a 100% stake in PGIM India Asset Management and its trustee business. PGIM India manages over ₹30,000 crore across 25 schemes as of December 2025. The deal, subject to regulatory approvals, marks a significant consolidation in the Indian asset management industry, which has reached ₹82.03 trillion in total assets.

articles.headline: Emami to acquire full control of Axiom Ayurveda for ₹200 crore
articles.summary: FMCG major Emami Ltd will acquire the remaining 73.5% stake in Axiom Ayurveda for a consideration not exceeding ₹200 crore. This gives Emami full control over the 'AloFrut' brand, which had a top line of ₹180 crore in FY26. The acquisition marks Emami's formal entry into the health foods segment and is expected to be completed in a phased manner over three months.

articles.headline: HAL Imposes Penalties on GE Aerospace for F404 Engine Delays
articles.summary: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited has invoked a penalty clause against GE Aerospace for delaying the supply of F404-IN20 engines for the Tejas LCA Mk-1A. Only 5 engines have been delivered against a promised 20 for the year. Liquidated damages, capped at 10% of the $716 million deal value, were imposed as the delay hinders production, though the existing IAF fleet will resume flying soon.

articles.headline: India achieves record ₹38,424 crore in defence exports for FY26
articles.summary: India's defence exports surged 63% to a record ₹38,424 crore in FY26, with hardware like BrahMos and Akash systems reaching 80 countries. Growth was driven by DPSUs and private firms like Bharat Forge and Tata Advanced Systems. The government aims to reach ₹50,000 crore in exports by 2029-30 as it integrates further into global supply chains.

articles.headline: RBI approves Emirates NBD acquisition of 74% stake in RBL Bank
articles.summary: The RBI has approved UAE-based Emirates NBD PJSC to acquire up to a 74% stake in RBL Bank for $3 billion, the largest foreign investment in a domestic bank. Under the 'no objection' deal, RBL will be reclassified as a foreign bank subsidiary, though voting rights are capped at 26%. Final approval from SEBI is pending for the move, which is expected to bring capital and global expertise.

articles.headline: Jaguar Land Rover reports 61% surge in fourth-quarter sales
articles.summary: Britain’s Jaguar Land Rover posted a 61.1% surge in fourth-quarter retail sales as production normalized following a prior cyber attack. Range Rover and Defender models accounted for 77.1% of sales. Despite the quarterly jump, full-year sales to dealers fell 23.2% due to production stoppages. JLR stated it has since strengthened its IT infrastructure to prevent future security breaches.

articles.headline: Bharti Airtel becomes world's second-largest telco by subscriber base
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel has become the world's second-largest mobile operator by reaching 650 million customers across India and Africa. According to GSMA Intelligence, the carrier has 368 million users in India and 179 million in Africa. The milestone follows investments in network expansion, including 5G Plus services and a foray into the non-banking financial sector.

articles.headline: IRDAI names LIC, New India, GIC Re as systemically important insurers
articles.summary: The IRDAI has re-identified Life Insurance Corporation (LIC), General Insurance Corporation (GIC Re), and New India Assurance as Domestic Systemically Important Insurers (D-SIIs) for FY26. Labeled "too big to fail," these entities are subject to enhanced regulatory supervision due to their critical role and interconnectedness within the national economy and financial system.

articles.headline: Amazon in talks to buy Globalstar for $9 billion
articles.summary: Amazon is reportedly in negotiations to acquire satellite telecom group Globalstar for approximately $9 billion to bolster its low-earth-orbit satellite internet business. The acquisition would provide critical orbital spectrum and assets to help Amazon compete with SpaceX’s Starlink. Globalstar shares surged 12.3% following reports of the potential merger.

articles.headline: Parliament Budget Session to resume on April 16 for seat expansion
articles.summary: The second half of the Parliament Budget session concluded after the passage of the CAPF Bill. Both Houses will reconvene on April 16 to address the women’s reservation bill and legislation to increase Lok Sabha seats from 543 to 816. While the government cites the expansion as necessary for reservation implementation, opposition parties argue it is intended for political advantage.

articles.headline: AAP replaces Raghav Chadha with Ashok Mittal as Rajya Sabha deputy leader
articles.summary: The Aam Aadmi Party has replaced Raghav Chadha with MP Ashok Mittal as its deputy leader in the Rajya Sabha. While the party called it a routine organizational process, the move follows reports of Chadha’s absence from the House and silence on party matters. The reshuffle aims to address leadership disconnect as the party prepares for upcoming assembly elections in Punjab and other states.

articles.headline: Mamata Banerjee alleges BJP plans to impose President’s Rule in Bengal
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee has alleged that the Malda siege incident was a BJP conspiracy to defame her government and create a pretext for imposing President’s Rule. She urged supporters not to be provoked by central agencies like the NIA. While the BJP accused the TMC of failing to protect judicial officers, Banerjee criticized the Election Commission's handling of poll violence.

articles.headline: NCERT granted deemed university status to offer doctoral and specialized degrees
articles.summary: The Union education ministry has notified the National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) as an "Institution deemed to be University." This status allows NCERT to independently offer its own undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral degrees. The move, aligned with the National Education Policy 2020, aims to strengthen teacher training and research across its institutes.

articles.headline: Emcure Pharmaceuticals slashes price of obesity drug Poviztra by 55%
articles.summary: Pune-based Emcure Pharmaceuticals has reduced the price of its semaglutide injection, Poviztra, by up to 55% to improve affordability. The starting 0.25 mg monthly dose will now cost ₹3,999, down from ₹8,790. This move follows similar price cuts by Novo Nordisk for Ozempic and Wegovy, reflecting increased competition in the Indian market for weight-management and diabetes treatments.

articles.headline: Karnataka approves expansion of Sneha Centres across health facilities
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has approved the expansion of Sneha Centres to all state health facilities, providing dedicated counselling and medical care for adolescents. Integrated units will be established in district and taluk hospitals to ensure better access and confidentiality. Following success in 14 districts, the initiative under the NHM aims to support youth mental and physical health.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes Jan Vishwas Bill to Decriminalize Minor Offences
articles.summary: Parliament has passed the Jan Vishwas (Amendment) Bill, 2026, which decriminalizes 784 provisions across 79 central laws to improve the ease of doing business. The legislation replaces criminal penalties with monetary fines for various procedural and technical lapses, reducing the burden on the judicial system and promoting a more business-friendly environment in India.

articles.headline: Chhattisgarh High Court convicts Amit Jogi in 2003 murder case
articles.summary: The Chhattisgarh High Court has convicted former MLA Amit Jogi, son of the state’s first CM Ajit Jogi, for the 2003 murder of NCP leader Ramavatar Jaggi. Setting aside a 2007 trial court acquittal, the bench found him guilty following a CBI plea. Jogi has been directed to surrender to serve his sentence, ending a long-running legal battle reopened by a Supreme Court directive.

articles.headline: Lokayukta police arrest two Bengaluru civic officials for accepting bribe
articles.summary: The Karnataka Lokayukta police arrested a revenue inspector and a tax inspector from the Greater Bengaluru Authority for allegedly accepting a ₹3 lakh bribe. The officials were caught in a trap operation in Thanisandra while demanding money from a resident to clear a pending 'Khata' application. Both officials have been taken into custody for further investigation into corruption charges.

articles.headline: Supreme Court deploys central forces following intimidation of West Bengal judges
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ordered the immediate deployment of central armed forces to protect judicial officers in West Bengal following the 'gherao' and intimidation of seven officers in Malda. Describing the event as a calculated attempt to demoralize the judiciary, the court slammed the state's inaction and directed officials to provide protection and an explanation for the security failure.

articles.headline: President Trump removes Attorney General Pam Bondi amid performance concerns
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has removed Pam Bondi as Attorney General, naming Deputy AG and former personal lawyer Todd Blanche as her acting replacement. White House officials cited frustration with Bondi's performance, particularly her handling of investigative files related to Jeffrey Epstein. While Bondi will reportedly transition to the private sector, the move was announced via social media.

articles.headline: Macron calls military operation to open Hormuz unrealistic
articles.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has stated that a military operation to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is 'unrealistic' and could expose vessels to ballistic missile risks. Emphasizing diplomacy over escalation, Macron urged for a negotiated ceasefire and consultations with Iran once hostilities cease. He warned that any military move would have catastrophic consequences for the global economy.

articles.headline: Nasa’s Artemis II crew successfully reaches orbit for lunar mission
articles.summary: NASA’s Artemis II mission, carrying four astronauts from the US and Canada, successfully reached orbit to begin a 10-day journey. The Orion capsule will perform a flyby of the moon's far side on April 6, traveling further than any humans in 50 years. The $30-billion mission is a critical test for establishing a long-term lunar base and planning eventual human voyages to Mars.

articles.headline: Indian women’s tennis team prepares for Billie Jean King Cup in Delhi
articles.summary: The Indian women’s tennis team has begun final preparations in Delhi for the Billie Jean King Cup Asia/Oceania Group I matches. Led by captain Vishal Uppal, the squad features Ankita Raina, Rutuja Bhosale, and Sahaja Yamalapalli. The team aims to leverage home-court advantage against competitors like Korea, Thailand, and Indonesia to secure a spot in the World Group playoffs for a second year.

articles.headline: Sunrisers Hyderabad dominant in 65-run victory over Kolkata Knight Riders
articles.summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad secured a 65-run victory over KKR, posting a massive 226/8 powered by Heinrich Klaasen’s quick-fire half-century and brisket forties from Abhishek Sharma and Travis Head. SRH's bowlers, led by Jaydev Unadkat, restricted KKR to 161 despite a 52-run knock from Angkrish Raghuvanshi. The win, SRH's first of the season, ended KKR's two-match winning streak.
